mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand, which compresses consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and industrial production indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, or the combined proxy correlation exceeds 0.95 rendering the interaction redundant.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate remains elevated, real interest rates turn positive and suppress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns specifically during high-inflation tightening regimes.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when real rates are deeply positive, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales figures reflect expanding consumer demand, which directly supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The forward return distribution for positive retail sales months does not differ significantly from the overall market average (t-test p > 0.05), indicating no predictive edge.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production drops below a threshold of 105, it signals a supply-side contraction and weakening factory output, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields a positive hit rate below 55% in walk-forward validation, or the signal frequency exceeds 70% of observations rendering it non-discriminatory.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The dual-labor signal fails to generate a positive information coefficient over a 3-year rolling window, or the proxies move inversely more than 30% of the time, breaking the joint premise.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion where GDP growth decelerates while housing starts remain elevated, the divergence signals an impending demand shock, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive equity returns in more than 50% of instances, or the cross-correlation between GDP growth and housing starts remains consistently positive, invalidating the divergence premise.
